references.text: [ErPu71] Erd\H{o}s, Paul and Purdy, George, Some extremal problems in geometry. J. Combinatorial Theory Ser. A (1971), 246--252.

remarks.text: A question of Erd\H{o}s and Purdy \cite{ErPu71}, who proved\[n^3\log n \ll f(n) \ll n^{7/2}.\]

statement.text: Let $f(n)$ be minimal such that every set of $n$ points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ contains at most $f(n)$ many sets of four points which are 'degenerate' in the sense that some pair are the same distance apart. Estimate $f(n)$ - in particular, is it true that $f(n)\leq n^{3+o(1)}$?
